# This file was autogenerated using quicktype https://github.com/quicktype/quicktype
#
# Add Jason to your mix.exs
#
# Decode a JSON string: User.from_json(data)
# Encode into a JSON string: User.to_json(struct)

defmodule Profile do
  @enforce_keys [:age, :active]
  defstruct [:age, :active]

  @type t :: %__MODULE__{
          age: integer(),
          active: boolean()
        }

  def from_map(m) do
    %Profile{
      age: m["age"],
      active: m["active"],
    }
  end

  def from_json(json) do
    json
          |> Jason.decode!()
          |> from_map()
  end

  def to_map(struct) do
    %{
      "age" => struct.age,
      "active" => struct.active,
    }
  end

  def to_json(struct) do
    struct
          |> to_map()
          |> Jason.encode!()
  end
end

defmodule User do
  @enforce_keys [:id, :name, :email, :roles, :profile]
  defstruct [:id, :name, :email, :roles, :profile]

  @type t :: %__MODULE__{
          id: integer(),
          name: String.t(),
          email: String.t(),
          roles: [String.t()],
          profile: Profile.t()
        }

  def from_map(m) do
    %User{
      id: m["id"],
      name: m["name"],
      email: m["email"],
      roles: m["roles"],
      profile: Profile.from_map(m["profile"]),
    }
  end

  def from_json(json) do
    json
          |> Jason.decode!()
          |> from_map()
  end

  def to_map(struct) do
    %{
      "id" => struct.id,
      "name" => struct.name,
      "email" => struct.email,
      "roles" => struct.roles,
      "profile" => Profile.to_map(struct.profile),
    }
  end

  def to_json(struct) do
    struct
          |> to_map()
          |> Jason.encode!()
  end
end
